beats.activity: Wakes up, showers, makes coffee and toast at home
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning wake-up; low energy (40/100) from yesterday suggests River needs a slow start. Gentle breakfast ritual at home before deciding on the day.

beats.activity: Browses vinyl records at home, checks camera equipment
beats.reasoning: River is a vinyl collector and carries a film camera everywhere; Sunday morning is perfect for pottering with hobbies before heading out. Low energy means no rush.

beats.activity: Drives to Pike Place Market to walk, photograph, and browse
beats.reasoning: Sunny Sunday with perfect 72F weather; River values good light for photography and would take advantage of the calm day to visit a favorite spot. About 30 min drive from Kirkland.

beats.activity: Walks through Pike Place Market, photographs vendors and architecture, samples local food
beats.reasoning: Extended leisure time on a sunny Sunday; River brings camera everywhere and enjoys the tactile, visual richness of the market. Naturally leads into lunch.

beats.activity: Lunch at a Pike Place vendor, sits by the waterfront
beats.reasoning: Generous with money, River treats himself to a leisurely lunch with a view on this perfect Sunday. No rush to leave.

beats.activity: Drives to Gas Works Park to photograph the lake and boats
beats.reasoning: Lake Union has heavy afternoon recreational boat traffic; River's camera and love of light make this an ideal afternoon stop. About 20 min drive from Pike Place.

beats.activity: Walks the park, photographs sailboats and paddle boards on Lake Union, sits on the hill
beats.reasoning: Perfect light and weather; River captures the busy afternoon boat traffic with film camera. Values observing people and nature over joining crowds—stays on the periphery.

beats.activity: Drives to Café Solstice in Wallingford for coffee and a snack
beats.reasoning: Still in the neighborhood; River is generous with money and enjoys a mid-afternoon coffee break. Gives him a comfortable spot to sit and review photos.

beats.activity: Browses a local used bookstore for vinyl or photography books
beats.reasoning: Easily distracted by interests; a bookstore walk-through fits the leisurely Sunday mood and his collecting habits. Short walk from café.
Afternoon adventures winding down; time to head home before evening. About 25 min drive from Wallingford back to Kirkland.

beats.activity: Prepares a simple dinner, organizes photos and film from the day, plays chess online; Listens to vinyl records, catalogs new finds, reads by the window as the sun sets
beats.reasoning: Low energy day transitioning to evening wind-down; River processes his day with hobbies and a quiet meal. Chess is a favorite way to decompress.
Full night of sleep after a gentle, leisure-filled Sunday. Ends well before 06:00 cutoff on Monday.

diary: Woke early with coffee and toast, then spent time with my vinyl collection—there's something grounding about those old records. Drove into the city around nine, felt drawn to Pike Place. The market was alive with people, light hitting the vendors' stalls just right for photography. I must've taken thirty shots of the flower stands alone. Grabbed lunch by the water, watched the ferries for a bit. Then over to Gas Works Park where Lake Union was packed with sailboats and paddleboards—that bright afternoon light made everything pop. The water was like glass. Stopped at Café Solstice for coffee and browsed the used bookstore, found a couple of old photography books that'll be good company. Back home now, organizing today's film, listening to one of my new finds spinning on the turntable. The house is quiet as the sun sets. Days like this remind me why I love this city.
thought_process: The weather pulled me outside—I knew it would be perfect for photography, and I wanted to make the most of the light. Pike Place felt right for a Sunday, less rushed than weekdays. I gravitated toward the waterfront and then Gas Works because I've been meaning to capture more of Lake Union's energy, especially with all those boats out. The whole day felt unhurried; I wasn't checking work emails or thinking about Monday. My mood stayed even and contemplative throughout. By evening, organizing the photos and settling with records felt like the natural conclusion—a way to process the day's moments. There's a rhythm to Sundays I need, especially as I get older: slow mornings, intentional wandering, then retreat back home to reflect.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
